title: Containers keep exiting randomly mid work
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Containers stop and exit randomly on their own even mid work and return to the host environment. 
```
root@cc94dee0e30c:/home# exit

[me@hostmachine ~]$ 
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a new container: `docker run -it ubuntu bash`

2. Container exits on its intermittently even mid work.

**Describe the results you received:**
Exit.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Container should not exit.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I have noticed this issue on x64 and PowerPC even when starting containers as root.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2
Go Version:   go1.14.7
Built:        Mon Feb  8 16:19:06 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
podman-2.2.1-7.module+el8.3.1+9857+68fb1526.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Is there anything on the system that may be sending a signal to these containers? My guess would be a process on the host is shooting either the container process or Conmon with SIGINT or SIGTERM and they are exiting because of this.

`docker run -it ubuntu bash`?
I take it you have podman-docker installed?

@rhatdan Yes it is using podman behind the scenes.

@mheon possibly, but I'm not aware of such process. Has this never happened in the past? it's strange as i'm experiencing it on 2 separate machines.

Yes we have never heard of it.  After podman executes the containers are just processes on the system.  Podman/Conmon are just monitoring them.  If they die, either they are dying naturally or something like systemd or the kernel is shooting them in the head.
I am going to close the issue.  If the issue persists, please use the official Red Hat customer channels which will give your issue priority.
